sentenceFrame: The star is in the dark.
celebration: Star debuts — decodable at its very first appearance (st + ar). ar is the first r-controlled sound and the cleanest: the r bends the a into /ar/ (star, car, park, farm). The Pattern Tree's first r-controlled leaf goes up.

songs.title: Ar-Ar-Star!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(ar… ar… ar…)
Ar! Ar! Star!

[Hook]
The R is BOSSY! (arrr!)
A plus R says /ar/! (arrr!)
Ar-ar-star! Ar-ar-star!
Two letters, one sound! (ar!)

[Verse 2]
S-T-A-R — star! (up in the dark!)
C-A-R — car! (beep! beep!)
Ar-ar-star! Ar-ar-star!
The bossy R says /ar/! (ar!)

[Verse 3]
Run in the park! (run! run!)
Down on the farm! (moo! moo!)
Ar-ar-star! Ar-ar-star!
The star is in the dark!

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! In the dark!
(ar… ar… ar…)

[Outro]
Star… car… park… (shhh…)
Ar! Ar! Star!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: First r-controlled song — growl the /ar/ (phonetic respelling: sung 'ar', printed ar), like a friendly pirate. The lesson is 'the bossy R' bending the a's sound. Spell-outs uppercase (S-T-A-R). Welcomes cast member Star (decodable at debut). Potato in the dark gag.

songs.title: The Star in the Dark
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The star is in the dark!
The star is in the dark!
Shine, star, shine! (way up high!)
The star is in the dark!

[Verse 2]
A car! A car! (beep! beep!)
The car is in the park!
Shine, star, shine! (way up high!)
Sheep can see the star!

[Verse 3]
Down on the farm! (moo! moo!)
The star is on the farm!
Shine, star, shine! (way up high!)
Bee can see the star!

[Outro]
The star… the dark… (shhh…)
In the dark?! (a potato!)
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The star is in the dark.' — Star is the new cast member; the song welcomes it warmly. 'see' carries the ee/ea team. A calm, glowing night song again — the r-controlled growl in the sound song, the tenderness here. Whisper the outro; potato hits loud under the star.

spreads.artNote: A wide, deep, velvety dark — the spotlight nearly out, just a hush of blue. The stage before the star.
CAST DEBUT: Star — a warm golden five-point star with a serene sleepy face — glowing softly in the dark, decodable at its very first appearance (st + ar).

spreads.text: The star is in the dark.
spreads.artNote: Star hanging alone in the dark sky, its gentle glow the only light, casting a soft gold across the treetops.
Sheep — fluffy round white sheep with kind dark eyes — lying on its back gazing up at Star, wool silvered, utterly content.
A little car with round headlamps rolling into the scene, beams sweeping the dark.

spreads.text: The car is in the park.
spreads.artNote: The little car parked under a tree in a quiet night park, Star reflected in its windshield.
A cosy farm at night — a red barn, a fence, a sleeping cow — under the same soft starlight.

spreads.text: The star is on the farm.
spreads.artNote: Star hanging low and warm right over the farm's barn, tucking the whole place in. Bee dozes on a fencepost.
Down in the dark grass, a single potato glows faintly gold, a tiny reflection of the star above it. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Star smiles sleepily down at it.

sentences: The star is in the dark.
The car is in the park.
The star is on the farm.
extras: THE FIRST R-CONTROLLED VOWEL — 'the bossy R.' When r follows a, it bends the a's sound into /ar/ (star, car, park, farm, arm, barn). The vowel doesn't say its own name and doesn't say its short sound — the r takes over. Growl it together.
WHY ar FIRST: of all the bossy-r sounds, ar is the cleanest and most reliable — one spelling, one sound. It opens the r-controlled unit before the trickier or (Week 48) and er/ir/ur (Week 49).
WELCOME STAR — decodable at debut (st + ar). Star joins the cast on the week its name becomes readable.
DO NOT introduce or, er, ir or ur yet — one bossy-r sound at a time. Hang the ar leaf on the Pattern Tree.
Moveable alphabet: build s-t-ar, c-ar, p-ar-k, f-ar-m — weld ar as one piece.

teacherFocus: THE FIRST R-CONTROLLED VOWEL — 'the bossy R.' When r follows a, it bends the a into /ar/: star, car, park, farm, arm, barn. The vowel gives up its own sound and lets the r take charge — growl it. ar comes first because it is the cleanest bossy-r: one spelling, one reliable sound. Welcome Star (decodable at debut). Teach ONE bossy-r sound at a time — do NOT introduce or/er/ir/ur yet. Hang the ar leaf.
